description: Curated catalog of biomedical research datasets Afferent can reason about. Facts (access, scope, modality) are verified; matching to a researcher is inference.

datasets.name: VitalDB
datasets.modality: High-resolution intraoperative waveforms + numerics, per-case clinical table, ±90-day labs, in-hospital outcomes
datasets.access: Open (no credentialing for the open subset)
datasets.strengths: Beat-to-beat physiology (BIS/EEG, arterial waveform, cardiac output) at a resolution few datasets match.
datasets.gaps: No ward course, no post-discharge follow-up, single-center; structured outcomes like delirium/POCD/30-day mortality are absent.

datasets.name: MIMIC-IV
datasets.modality: De-identified ICU + ED EHR: vitals, labs, meds, notes, billing, outcomes
datasets.strengths: Rich longitudinal ICU course with outcomes, labs, meds, and clinical notes; the workhorse for ICU ML.
datasets.gaps: No high-frequency waveforms (see MIMIC-IV-WDB / matched waveform subset); single-center; US ICU case-mix.

datasets.name: eICU-CRD
datasets.modality: Multi-center ICU EHR (208 US hospitals): vitals, labs, diagnoses, treatments, APACHE
datasets.strengths: Multi-center generalizability for ICU prediction; good for external validation of MIMIC models.
datasets.gaps: Coarser granularity than MIMIC; no notes; no high-frequency waveforms.

datasets.name: HiRID
datasets.modality: High-resolution ICU data (~2-min resolution) for ~33,000 admissions
datasets.strengths: Higher temporal resolution than MIMIC/eICU for circulatory-failure and early-warning work.
datasets.gaps: Single-center European ICU; smaller; no notes.

datasets.name: MOVER
datasets.modality: Perioperative EHR + intraoperative vitals for ~58,000 surgical patients
datasets.access: Open with registration / data use agreement
datasets.strengths: Large perioperative cohort linking intraoperative data to EHR outcomes — a complement/scale-up to VitalDB.
datasets.gaps: Lower waveform fidelity than VitalDB; single-center US.

datasets.name: NHANES
datasets.modality: Nationally representative US survey: exams, labs, questionnaires, mortality linkage
datasets.strengths: Population-representative exposures/biomarkers with mortality linkage — gold standard for US epi.
datasets.gaps: Cross-sectional cycles (limited longitudinal); self-report measures; no clinical course.

datasets.name: BRFSS
datasets.modality: Annual US telephone survey of health behaviors and conditions
datasets.strengths: Huge, geographically resolved behavioral-risk surveillance for population health.
datasets.gaps: All self-report; no biomarkers or clinical data.

datasets.name: ABCD Study
datasets.modality: Longitudinal adolescent brain development: MRI, cognition, mental health, substance use, genetics
datasets.strengths: The premier longitudinal adolescent neurodevelopment cohort with imaging + behavior + genetics.
datasets.gaps: Access requires an approved NDA application; complex multilevel structure; not clinical-care data.

datasets.name: Healthy Brain Network
datasets.modality: Pediatric mental-health phenotyping: MRI, EEG, cognitive/behavioral assessments
datasets.strengths: Deep transdiagnostic pediatric phenotyping with imaging + EEG; relatively accessible.

datasets.name: UK Biobank
datasets.modality: 500k-participant biobank: genotyping, imaging, biomarkers, linked health records
datasets.strengths: Genetics + imaging + longitudinal linkage at scale — unmatched for gene-environment and risk-prediction work.
datasets.gaps: Healthy-volunteer bias; application + fee; UK population.
